name: Umar ibn Hasan
title: Hasan's Orphan Child
shortDescription: The young child of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS) who was present in Karbala and was taken to Sham as a captive.
fullStory:
Umar ibn Hasan ibn Ali (AS) was another legacy of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS) in Karbala. He was a young child (or early teenager) during the Ashura event.

Umar, along with his brothers (Qasim, Abdullah, Abu Bakr, and Amr), was present in his uncle Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan. While his older brothers were martyred in the battlefield, Umar remained in the tents due to his young age.

After the tents were looted, he was taken captive along with the women and other children. Ibn Sa'd sent him and the other captives to Ibn Ziyad in Kufa and then to Yazid in Sham.

In Yazid's court, Yazid, upon seeing this young child (Umar ibn Hasan), mockingly said to him: 'Are you willing to wrestle with my son Khalid?' Umar bravely replied: 'Give each of us a knife so we can fight; if he kills me, I will join my grandfather, and if I kill him, he will join his ancestors in the fire.' This decisive answer embarrassed Yazid.
quoteArabic: أَعْطِنِي سِكِّيناً وَأَعْطِهِ سِكِّيناً حَتّى أُقاتِلَهُ...
quote: (The child's brave reply to Yazid:) Give me a knife and give him a knife so I can fight him...
